# W24T2 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal
Implement the supported `CastTo<T>` matrix in `src/convert/cast.rs`. Every supported source and target pair must follow the documented success, default-error, and conditional-success rules.

## Related Design Documents
- `design/21-type.md` §5.3, §5.4, §6.1, §8.2, §8.8, §10
- `design/03-element.md` §5.9
- `design/01-architecture.md` §1.5
- `design/00-coding.md` §2.3, §2.5, §2.7, §3.1

## Dependencies
- W24T1
- W5, `Complex<f32>` and `Complex<f64>`
- The public `XenonError::TypeConversion` payload from the error module

## Files to Create/Modify
- `src/convert/cast.rs`

## Implementation Details
### Trait method signature for every impl
```rust
impl CastTo<Target> for Source {
    fn cast_to(self) -> Result<Target, XenonError>;
}
```

- `element_index` must remain `None` inside `CastTo<T>` impls. The caller sets it later.
- Build the full matrix through macros or exhaustive impl blocks.

### Supported success-by-default pairs
- `i32 -> i64`
- `f32 -> f64`
- `i32 -> f64`
- `i32 -> Complex<f64>`
- `f32 -> Complex<f64>`
- `f32 -> Complex<f32>`
- `f64 -> Complex<f64>`
- `Complex<f32> -> Complex<f64>`

### Supported default-error pairs
- `i64 -> i32`
- `f64 -> f32`
- `f32 -> i32`
- `f32 -> i64`
- `f64 -> i32`
- `f64 -> i64`
- `i32 -> f32`
- `i64 -> f32`
- `i64 -> f64`
- `i32 -> Complex<f32>`
- `i64 -> Complex<f64>`
- `i64 -> Complex<f32>`
- `f64 -> Complex<f32>`
- `Complex<f64> -> Complex<f32>`

### Supported conditional-success pairs
- `Complex<f32> -> f64`
- `Complex<f32> -> f32`
- `Complex<f32> -> i32`
- `Complex<f32> -> i64`
- `Complex<f64> -> f64`
- `Complex<f64> -> f32`
- `Complex<f64> -> i32`
- `Complex<f64> -> i64`

Conditional rules:
- Complex to real starts by requiring `imag == 0`.
- That check is necessary, not sufficient.
- After `imag == 0`, the inner real conversion must still follow the same lossless or lossy rule from `21-type.md` §5.3.
- Any lossy step returns `XenonError::TypeConversion`.

### Closure rules that must be encoded in the implementation
- Real to complex, convert the real part first, then add zero imaginary part.
- Complex to real, require zero imaginary part, then apply the inner real conversion rule.
- Complex to complex, convert both parts independently under the matching real conversion rule.
- Any lossy inner step makes the whole conversion fail.

## Test Plan
- `test_cast_f64_to_f32_returns_error`
- `test_cast_f32_to_f64`
- `test_cast_float_to_int_returns_error`
- `test_cast_nan_to_int_returns_error`
- `test_cast_inf_to_int_returns_error`
- `test_cast_int_narrowing_returns_error`
- `test_cast_real_to_complex`
- `test_cast_complex_to_real_requires_zero_imag`
- `test_cast_complex_to_int_requires_zero_imag_and_inner_success`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every documented source and target pair is implemented.
- No undocumented element type enters the matrix.
- `bool` is excluded completely.
- Conditional complex conversions require zero imaginary part and still honor inner lossiness rules.
- All conversion impls return `Result<T, XenonError>` and leave `element_index` unset.
- The compile-time matrix is complete, with no missing documented pair.

## Development Workflow
1. Write failing tests for one success case, one lossy failure, and one complex conditional case.
2. Implement the matrix with macros or exhaustive impls.
3. Add the remaining documented pair tests.
4. Run the conversion tests once and stop after the first clean pass.
